titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.13

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=85 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:15:58.89095+00 | translation moa=86 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:16:22.509188+00

div.p:
W dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue
Clericis et Laicis Salutem . Sciatis me pro anima mea et pro animabus patris et matris mee . et Aui mei regis D et fratrum
et sororum . et Antecessorum . et Successorum meorum . concessisse et dedisse Deo et ecclesie Sancte marie de melr’ et mona
chis ibidem deo seruientibus . de Rieualle . in perpetuam et liberam elemosinam terram de Melros . et terram de El
dune . et terram de Dernewich . libere et honorifice perpetuo tenore possidere . Insuper sciatis me dedisse et conces
sisse eis per totum forestum meum de seleschirche et de treuequair Aisiamenta sua . Scilicet ligna . materiem et pasna
gium . ad suos proprios usus ubicumque sibi melius et vtilius poterunt inuenire . Insuper do eis et confirmo totam pa
sturam per fluuium Galche uersus eos scilicet ab orientali parte . sicut idem fluuius currit . usque ad terminos de
Wedale . et inde per rectas diuisas terre Ricardi de Moreuilla scilicet de Galche usque ad leddre et totum usagi
um nemoris infra prenominatas diuisas ad suos proprios usus sicut opus eis fuerit . Preterea et in aquis de Twe
da infra terminos eorum piscaturam tam ex mea parte fluminis quam ex eorum parte ubique . Et preter supradicta de
di et concessi eis ad incrementum Galtuneside per has diuisas . scilicet sicut fluuius cadit in Thweda . et ita
sursum usque ad Burnam de fachope que cadit in leddre . Et ita sursum per eandem burnam . et sic per transuersum
moram usque ad Raburne que cadit in Aloent . et sic per eundem fluuium de Aloent usque in Thwedam .
Preter omnia prenominata dedi et Concessie eis in inferiori parte de Cumbesleia locum ad edificandam unam
uaccariam centum uaccarum . et unam Faldam . Dedi et concessi eis piscariam de Seleschirche . et unum rete
in Berewichstrem . de duobus retibus meis que habui in Berewichstrem . ita libere et quiete sicut ipse melius
tenui ad opus meum . Hec autem omnia predictis monachis de Melr’ do et concedo . et hac Carta mea con
firmo . in perpetuam et liberam elemosinam . et uolo et precipio ut omnia supradicta ita libere et quiete .
plenarie et honorifice teneant . sicut carta Regis Malcolmi fratris mei testatur et confirmat . His testibus .
Waltero Bidun Cancellario . Comite Dunecano . Ricardo de Morevuilla constabulario . Ricardo Cumin . Iusticia .
Roberto auenel . Waltero de Berchelaie Camerario . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Waltero Ridale . Hugone clerico re
gis . Willelmo capellano . Roberto Capellano . Hugone clerico Cancellarii . Magistro Iohanne . Comite Waltheuo . Wal
tero filio Alani . Dapifero . Alano filio eius . Thoma de Muscamp . Willelmo de la haie . Radulfo ruffo .
APUD Rokesburch .

W(illiam), by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Know me, for the sake of my own soul and for the souls of my father and my mother, and of my grandfather King David, and of my brothers and sisters, and predecessors and successors, to have conceded and given to God, to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and the monks of Rievaulx serving God in that place, in everlasting and free alms, the land of Melrose, and the land of Eildon, and the land of Darnick, to possess freely and honourably in perpetual tenure. In addition, know me to have given and conceded to them their easements throughout my whole forest of Selkirk and of Traquhair, that is to say, firewood, timber, and pannage, for their own use wherever they will the better and more usefully be able to find for themselves. In addition I give and make firm the entire pasture by the River Gala towards them, that is, from the eastern side, as the same river flows, as far as the limits of Wedale, and from there by the right marches of Richard de Morville's land, that is, from the Gala as far as the Leader, and the whole usage of the wood within the aforenamed boundaries for their own employment as might be necessary for them. Furthermore, fishing also in the waters of the Tweed, anywhere inside their boundaries, as much on my side of the river as on their side. And in addition to the things mentioned above I have given and conceded to them, as an augmentation, Gattonside by these boundaries, namely, as the river fall into the Tweed, and so upwards to the Fauhope burn which falls into the Leader, and so upwards by the same burn and thus across the moor as far as the Rae burn which falls into the Allan, and so following the same river Allan as far as the Tweed. Besides all the aforenamed things I have given and conceded to them on the lower side of Colmslie a place for building one cow-shed for one hundred cows, and one cattle-fold. I have given and conceded to them the fishery of Selkirk, and one net in Berwick Stream from my two nets which I have in Berwick Stream, so freely and quitly as I myself held for my own use. All these aforementioned things I give and concede to the monks of Melrose, and make firm by this my charter, in everlasting and free alms, and I will and instruct that they may hold all the things mentioned above as freely and quitly, fully and honourably as the charter of my brother King Malcolm bears witness and affirms. With these as witnesses: Walter Bidun, chancellor; Earl Duncan; Richard de Morville, constable; Richard Comyn, justice; Robert Avenel; Walter of Berkeley, chamberlain; William de Somerville; Walter Ryedale; Hugh the king's clerk; William the chaplain; Robert the chaplain; Hugh the chancellor's clerk; Master John; Earl Waldef; Walter son of Alan, steward; Alan his son; Thomas de Muschamp; William de la Haye; Ralph Ruffus. At Roxburgh.
